topic: In China employee termination: myth-vs-fact guide for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination myths and facts foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Primary source for correcting misconceptions about route legality and consequences.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports practical interpretation of route handling and implementation boundaries.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Grounds myth-corrections related to evidentiary and dispute-facing risk.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors myth corrections linked to statutory closure and contribution obligations.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides practical foreign-employer context to translate legal facts into operational meaning.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from position-paper format to myth-vs-fact format while preserving legal-source hierarchy.
Excluded lower-fit tax-centric and broad comparative sources to keep misconception correction precise.
Retained China Briefing as practical translation layer for non-local readers.
consensus: Foreign employers often carry at-will assumptions that conflict with China route-based termination logic.
Myth-vs-fact framing improves comprehension when each correction is tied to source-backed legal context.
Cross-function consistency remains the dominant practical control once myths are corrected.
uncertainties: City-level implementation and evidentiary emphasis vary.
Some payment-component treatments in edge cases may require local confirmation.
Official English texts may not capture every Chinese-language implementation nuance.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for myth-vs-fact format because sources support high-confidence legal corrections plus practical multinational interpretation.
